headline: Nasdaq Logs Longest Winning Streak Since 2021 as Investors Look Beyond War
summary: Oil prices have retreated.  The prospect of a peace deal that reopens the Strait of Hormuz has now powered the Nasdaq composite to its longest streak of daily gains since November 2021—a 10-session ascent that has lifted the tech-heavy index 14%.  The Dow Jones Industrial Average’s energy-hungry components have the blue-chip index lagging behind other major benchmarks, on Tuesday notching a 0.7% gain.

content.title: Nasdaq Logs Longest Winning Streak Since 2021 as Investors Look Beyond War

headline: C Q1 Deep Dive: Services Strength and Transformation Progress Drive Outperformance
summary: Global financial services giant Citigroup (NYSE:C) reported Q1 CY2026 results exceeding the market’s revenue expectations, with sales up 13.9% year on year to $24.63 billion. Its non-GAAP profit of $3.06 per share was 16.4% above analysts’ consensus estimates.

content.title: C Q1 Deep Dive: Services Strength and Transformation Progress Drive Outperformance
content.summary: Global financial services giant Citigroup (NYSE:C) reported Q1 CY2026 results exceeding the market’s revenue expectations, with sales up 13.9% year on year to $24.63 billion. Its non-GAAP profit of $3.06 per share was 16.4% above analysts’ consensus estimates.

headline: Oil Holds Loss on Prospect of Fresh Peace Talks With Hormuz Shut
summary: (Bloomberg) -- Oil largely held onto a sharp drop from this week’s highs as the US and Iran seek further talks to end a war that has brought the vital Strait of Hormuz waterway to a near-halt. Brent traded around $96 a barrel, roughly back to its level before peace talks broke down over the weekend, after losing almost 5% on Tuesday. headline: Like Other Bank CEOs, BofA's Moynihan Says U.S. Economy Is ‘Resilient’
summary: Bank of America Chief Executive Officer Brian Moynihan said the U.S. economy is “resilient,” joining a chorus of bank CEOs who see similar strength in the face of geopolitical tensions.  “We remain watchful of evolving risks,” Moynihan, who runs the second-largest U.S. lender, said in a statement.  “However, we saw healthy client activity, including solid consumer spending and stable asset quality, indicating a resilient American economy.”

content.title: Like Other Bank CEOs, BofA's Moynihan Says U.S. Economy Is ‘Resilient’
content.summary: Bank of America Chief Executive Officer Brian Moynihan said the U.S. economy is “resilient,” joining a chorus of bank CEOs who see similar strength in the face of geopolitical tensions.  “We remain watchful of evolving risks,” Moynihan, who runs the second-largest U.S. lender, said in a statement.  “However, we saw healthy client activity, including solid consumer spending and stable asset quality, indicating a resilient American economy.”

headline: Bank of America’s Profit Increases 17%
summary: Bank of America said its profit rose 17% from a year earlier in the first quarter.    Net income came in at $8.58 billion. That amounted to $1.11 a share, above analyst estimates of $1.01.   Revenue was 7% higher at $30.

content.title: Bank of America’s Profit Increases 17%
content.summary: Bank of America said its profit rose 17% from a year earlier in the first quarter.    Net income came in at $8.58 billion. That amounted to $1.11 a share, above analyst estimates of $1.01.   Revenue was 7% higher at $30.

headline: Hedge Funds Pivot to Bearish Dollar Bets on US-Iran Talks Optimism
summary: (Bloomberg) -- Hedge funds are increasingly downbeat on the dollar as the prospect of renewed US-Iran talks and a possible peace deal sap the currency’s war-driven strength. Investors added to their bearish dollar trades this month through April 10, based on a proprietary trading model from Morgan Stanley.In the options market, so-called risk reversals on the Bloomberg dollar index show the premium to hedge against a stronger dollar compared with bets against a weaker greenback has narrowed this

headline: Oil Fluctuates on Prospect of Fresh Peace Talks With Hormuz Shut
summary: (Bloomberg) -- Oil flipped between gains and losses as traders weighed signs of rapidly depleting US reserves against efforts by the US and Iran to seek further talks to end a war that has brought transit through the vital Strait of Hormuz waterway to a near-halt. West Texas Intermediate traded around $92 a barrel, roughly back to its level before peace negotiations broke down over the weekend, after losing almost 8% on Tuesday. The US and Iran are moving closer to a deal to extend the current c
